name: Mexican Mocha Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle gelatin over 1/2 cup cold water in a measuring cup; let soften for about 5 minutes.
2. Separate the eggs: place 2 egg whites in a small bowl with an electric mixer and 4 egg yolks in the top of a double boiler (refrigerate remaining whites for another recipe).
3. Let egg whites warm to room temperature.
4. Beat yolks slightly with a wooden spoon; stir in gelatin mixture, semisweet chocolate, and 2 tablespoons sugar.
5. Cook over hot, not boiling water until melted, about 10 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in coffee flavored liqueur.
6. Pour the chocolate mixture into a medium-sized bowl; place it in a large bowl of ice water. Stir occasionally until it reaches the consistency of beaten egg whites, about 15 minutes.
7. Toast almonds: melt butter in a small skillet over medium heat, add almonds, and cook, stirring, just until golden brown. Cool.
8. In a chilled bowl of an electric mixer, whip heavy cream until stiff; refrigerate.
9. Meanwhile, with the electric mixer (using clean beaters), beat egg whites until soft peaks form when beaters are slowly raised.
10. Gradually beat in remaining 2 tablespoons sugar, beating until stiff peaks form when beaters are raised.
11. Using a wire whisk or rubber spatula, fold beaten egg whites and 1 1/2 cups whipped cream into the chocolate mixture. Cover and refrigerate any leftover whipped cream.
12. Turn filling into pie shell, spreading evenly. Refrigerate until filling is firm, about 3 hours.
13. Chop toasted almonds. Pipe remaining whipped cream along the inside edge of the pie crust using a pastry bag fitted with a #6 tip.
14. Sprinkle almonds around the inside edge of the whipped cream.
15. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
Variation: Add 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon to the chocolate mixture.
Note: Exercise caution when preparing recipes that include raw eggs to avoid salmonella exposure.
